title: Maine Lobster Roll New England

seo.title: Best Maine Lobster Roll Recipe | New England Classic
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of the perfect Maine Lobster Roll! This authentic New England recipe features fresh lobster, a creamy dressing, and a toasted bun. Get the best homemade taste!
longTailKeywords: how to make Maine Lobster Roll
authentic Maine Lobster Roll New England style
best Maine Lobster Roll recipe at home

traditional Maine lobster roll with mayo
lobster roll with brioche bun recipe
seo.h1: The Best Maine Lobster Roll Recipe: A New England Classic
h2Headings: What is a Maine Lobster Roll?
Ingredients You'll Need for Your Lobster Roll
Step-by-Step: How to Make a Maine Lobster Roll

seo.imageAlt: A classic Maine lobster roll on a toasted bun, filled with fresh lobster meat and a light dressing, garnished with chives.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the taste of the coast with our authentic Maine Lobster Roll recipe, a true New England delight!

description: Imagine biting into the quintessential taste of New England with this classic Maine Lobster Roll. Succulent, sweet lobster meat is lightly dressed in a creamy, tangy sauce, nestled in a perfectly toasted, buttery split-top bun. This iconic American seafood delight brings the essence of the Maine coast right to your plate, offering a refreshing and luxurious culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: from 2-3 lobsters, chilled and cut into bite-sized pieces
freshly chopped, plus extra for garnish

ingredients.item: New England style hot dog buns

instructions.title: Prepare the Lobster Meat
instructions.text: If using whole live lobsters, steam or boil them until fully cooked (about 8-10 minutes for 1.25 lb lobsters). Let them cool completely, then extract the meat from the tails, claws, and knuckles. Cut the lobster meat into large, bite-sized chunks, ensuring not to shred it too finely. Chill the prepared lobster meat thoroughly in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Make the Creamy Dressing
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the mayonnaise, fresh lemon juice, and chopped chives. If using, add the finely diced celery. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Whisk until all ingredients are well combined and the dressing is smooth.

instructions.title: Combine Lobster and Dressing
instructions.text: Gently fold the chilled lobster meat into the creamy dressing. Be careful not to overmix, as this can break down the delicate lobster texture. The goal is to lightly coat the lobster, allowing its natural sweetness to shine through. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.

instructions.title: Chill the Lobster Salad
instructions.text: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the lobster salad for at least 15-20 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld together and ensures the lobster roll is served refreshingly cold, which is traditional for the Maine style.

instructions.title: Prepare and Toast the Buns
instructions.text: While the lobster salad chills, prepare your buns. Lightly brush the outsides of each New England style hot dog bun with melted unsalted butter. Heat a large skillet or griddle over medium-low heat. Place the buttered buns flat-side down on the skillet and toast for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and slightly crispy. The split-top buns should be toasted on both flat sides.

instructions.title: Assemble the Lobster Rolls
instructions.text: Carefully open each toasted bun. Generously fill each bun with the chilled lobster salad, mounding it high. The lobster should be the star of the show. You can use a spoon or a small spatula to help fill them neatly.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish each Maine Lobster Roll with a sprinkle of fresh chopped chives for color and a hint of oniony flavor. Serve immediately with potato chips, coleslaw, or a side of pickles for a truly authentic New England experience.

tips: For the most authentic Maine Lobster Roll, use a high-quality, full-fat mayonnaise and fresh lemon juice. Avoid anything low-fat or pre-bottled for the best flavor.
Always chill your lobster meat and the assembled salad thoroughly before serving. The cold temperature is key to the refreshing taste of a classic Maine-style lobster roll.
New England style split-top buns are crucial. If unavailable, you can carefully slice the top of a regular hot dog bun lengthwise without cutting all the way through, then toast the sides.
article: Maine Lobster Roll New England is a beloved American Seafood dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
